---
title: "House Price Prediction"
author: "Nabil Manzo"
date: "2026-06-12"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r load-data, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(AmesHousing)
library(tidyverse)

ames <- ames_raw

dim(ames)
head(ames, 3)
glimpse(ames)
```
```{r}
# Clean column names
library(janitor)
ames <- ames_raw %>% clean_names()
names(ames)
```

```{r}
# Missing value assessment
missing_summary <- ames %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~sum(is.na(.)))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "column", values_to = "missing") %>%
  filter(missing > 0) %>%
  mutate(pct_missing = round(missing / nrow(ames) * 100, 1)) %>%
  arrange(desc(missing))

print(missing_summary, n = 30)
```
```{r}
# Data Cleaning
ames_clean <- ames %>%
  bind_cols(make_ames() %>% select(Longitude, Latitude)) %>%
  rename(longitude = Longitude, latitude = Latitude) %>%

  # 1. Drop identifier columns — no predictive value
  select(-order,-pid) %>%
  
  # 2. Drop rows with genuine missing data (tiny NAs)
  drop_na(electrical, bsmt_full_bath, bsmt_half_bath, 
          bsmt_fin_sf_1, bsmt_fin_sf_2, bsmt_unf_sf,
          total_bsmt_sf, garage_cars, garage_area) %>%
  
  # 3. NAs that mean "feature doesn't exist" — replace with "None"
  mutate(
    alley        = replace_na(alley, "None"),
    fence        = replace_na(fence, "None"),
    misc_feature = replace_na(misc_feature, "None"),
    fireplace_qu = replace_na(fireplace_qu, "None"),
    pool_qc      = replace_na(pool_qc, "None"),
    garage_type      = replace_na(garage_type, "None"),
    garage_finish    = replace_na(garage_finish, "None"),
    garage_qual      = replace_na(garage_qual, "None"),
    garage_cond      = replace_na(garage_cond, "None"),
    bsmt_qual        = replace_na(bsmt_qual, "None"),
    bsmt_cond        = replace_na(bsmt_cond, "None"),
    bsmt_exposure    = replace_na(bsmt_exposure, "None"),
    bsmt_fin_type_1  = replace_na(bsmt_fin_type_1, "None"),
    bsmt_fin_type_2  = replace_na(bsmt_fin_type_2, "None"),
    mas_vnr_type     = replace_na(mas_vnr_type, "None"),
    mas_vnr_area     = replace_na(mas_vnr_area, 0)
  ) %>%
  
  # 4a. Impute lot_frontage using median by neighborhood + lot_config
  group_by(neighborhood, lot_config) %>%
  mutate(lot_frontage = ifelse(
    is.na(lot_frontage),
    median(lot_frontage, na.rm = TRUE),
    lot_frontage
  )) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  
  # 4b. Fallback for any remaining lot_frontage NAs
mutate(lot_frontage = ifelse(
    is.na(lot_frontage),
    median(lot_frontage, na.rm = TRUE),
    lot_frontage
  )) %>%
  
  # 5. Fix garage_yr_blt — NA means no garage, replace with 0
  mutate(garage_yr_blt = replace_na(garage_yr_blt, 0))

# Verify no missing values remain
sum(is.na(ames_clean))

# 6. Remove non-typical sales and outliers per dataset author's recommendation
ames_clean <- ames_clean %>%
  filter(sale_condition == "Normal") %>%
  filter(gr_liv_area <= 4000)

```

```{r}
dim(ames_clean)
```


```{r}
# Target variable — Sale Price distribution
library(scales)

summary(ames_clean$sale_price)
```

```{r}
# Boxplot of sale price distribution
ggplot(ames_clean, aes(x = "", y = sale_price)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "#4299e1", color = "#2b6cb0", alpha = 0.7) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point",
               shape = 18, size = 4, color = "red") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = dollar_format()) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Sale Prices",
    subtitle = paste0("Median: $", format(median(ames_clean$sale_price), big.mark = ","),
                      " | Mean: $", format(round(mean(ames_clean$sale_price)), big.mark = ","),
                      " (red diamond)"),
    x = "",
    y = "Sale Price"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank())
```

```{r}
# Sale price distribution with normal curve
ggplot(ames_clean, aes(x = sale_price)) +
  geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), 
                 bins = 50, fill = "#4299e1", color = "white") +
  stat_function(
    fun = dnorm,
    args = list(mean = mean(ames_clean$sale_price), 
                sd = sd(ames_clean$sale_price)),
    color = "red",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = dollar_format()) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Sale Prices",
    subtitle = "Red curve shows what a perfect normal distribution would look like",
    x = "Sale Price",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# Log transformed sale price
ggplot(ames_clean, aes(x = log(sale_price))) +
  geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)),
                 bins = 50, fill = "#48bb78", color = "white") +
  stat_function(
    fun = dnorm,
    args = list(mean = mean(log(ames_clean$sale_price)),
                sd = sd(log(ames_clean$sale_price))),
    color = "red",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Log(Sale Price)",
    subtitle = "Log transformation brings the distribution closer to normal",
    x = "Log(Sale Price)",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# Boxplot of log sale price distribution
ggplot(ames_clean, aes(x = "", y = log(sale_price))) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "#48bb78", color = "#276749", alpha = 0.7) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point",
               shape = 18, size = 4, color = "red") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Log(Sale Price)",
    subtitle = paste0("Median: ", round(median(log(ames_clean$sale_price)), 2),
                      " | Mean: ", round(mean(log(ames_clean$sale_price)), 2),
                      " (red diamond)"),
    x = "",
    y = "Log(Sale Price)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank())
```


```{r}
# Neighborhood name mapping
neighborhood_names <- c(
  Blmngtn = "Bloomington Heights",
  Blueste = "Bluestem",
  BrDale = "Briardale",
  BrkSide = "Brookside",
  ClearCr = "Clear Creek",
  CollgCr = "College Creek",
  Crawfor = "Crawford",
  Edwards = "Edwards",
  Gilbert = "Gilbert",
  Greens = "Greens",
  GrnHill = "Green Hills",
  IDOTRR = "Iowa DOT and Rail Road",
  Landmrk = "Landmark",
  MeadowV = "Meadow Village",
  Mitchel = "Mitchell",
  NAmes = "North Ames",
  NoRidge = "Northridge",
  NPkVill = "Northpark Villa",
  NridgHt = "Northridge Heights",
  NWAmes = "Northwest Ames",
  OldTown = "Old Town",
  SWISU = "South & West of Iowa State University",
  Sawyer = "Sawyer",
  SawyerW = "Sawyer West",
  Somerst = "Somerset",
  StoneBr = "Stone Brook",
  Timber = "Timberland",
  Veenker = "Veenker"
)

# Apply mapping to ames_clean
ames_clean <- ames_clean %>%
  mutate(neighborhood = recode(neighborhood, !!!neighborhood_names))

# Sale price by neighborhood
ames_clean %>%
  group_by(neighborhood) %>%
  summarise(median_price = median(sale_price),
            n = n()) %>%
  arrange(median_price) %>%
  mutate(neighborhood = factor(neighborhood, levels = neighborhood)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = neighborhood, y = median_price)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#4299e1") +
  geom_text(aes(label = dollar(median_price)), 
            hjust = -0.1, size = 3) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = dollar_format(), 
                     limits = c(0, 350000)) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Median Sale Price by Neighborhood",
    subtitle = "Neighborhoods ordered from least to most expensive",
    x = "",
    y = "Median Sale Price"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
```{r}
ames_clean %>%
  group_by(neighborhood) %>%
  summarise(median_price = median(sale_price)) %>%
  arrange(median_price) %>%
  slice(c(1, n()))
```
```{r}
ames_clean %>%
  group_by(neighborhood) %>%
  summarise(median_price = median(sale_price)) %>%
  arrange(median_price) %>%
  print(n = 28)
```

```{r}
# Mathematically cluster neighborhoods into tiers
neighborhood_prices <- ames_clean %>%
  group_by(neighborhood) %>%
  summarise(median_price = median(sale_price))

# Apply k-means with 4 clusters
set.seed(42)
kmeans_result <- kmeans(neighborhood_prices$median_price, centers = 4)

neighborhood_prices <- neighborhood_prices %>%
  mutate(tier_num = kmeans_result$cluster)

# See what k-means found
neighborhood_prices %>%
  arrange(median_price) %>%
  print(n = 28)
```
```{r}
# Assign meaningful tier names
neighborhood_prices <- neighborhood_prices %>%
  mutate(neighborhood_tier = case_when(
    tier_num == 4 ~ "Budget",
    tier_num == 1 ~ "Mid-Range",
    tier_num == 3 ~ "Premium",
    tier_num == 2 ~ "Luxury"
  ))

# Join tiers back to ames_clean
ames_clean <- ames_clean %>%
  left_join(neighborhood_prices %>% 
              select(neighborhood, neighborhood_tier), 
            by = "neighborhood")
ames_clean <- ames_clean %>%
  mutate(neighborhood_tier = factor(neighborhood_tier, 
                                    levels = c("Budget", "Mid-Range", "Premium", "Luxury")))

# Verify
ames_clean %>% count(neighborhood_tier)
```


```{r}
ames_clean %>%
  group_by(neighborhood, neighborhood_tier) %>%
  summarise(median_price = median(sale_price), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(median_price) %>%
  mutate(neighborhood = factor(neighborhood, levels = neighborhood)) %>%
  mutate(neighborhood_tier = factor(neighborhood_tier, 
                                   levels = c("Budget", "Mid-Range", "Premium", "Luxury"))) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = neighborhood, y = median_price, fill = neighborhood_tier)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label = dollar(median_price)), hjust = -0.1, size = 3) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = dollar_format(), limits = c(0, 350000)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Budget"    = "#fc8181",
    "Mid-Range" = "#4299e1",
    "Premium"   = "#48bb78",
    "Luxury"    = "#ed8936"
  )) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Median Sale Price by Neighborhood",
    subtitle = "Colored by market tier — determined by k-means clustering",
    x = "",
    y = "Median Sale Price",
    fill = "Tier"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# Living area vs sale price
ggplot(ames_clean, aes(x = gr_liv_area, y = sale_price)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, color = "#4299e1") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "red", se = FALSE) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = dollar_format()) +
  labs(
    title = "Sale Price vs Above Ground Living Area",
    subtitle = "Each point represents one house sale",
    x = "Above Ground Living Area (sq ft)",
    y = "Sale Price"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```


```{r}
ggplot(ames_clean, aes(x = gr_liv_area, y = sale_price, color = neighborhood_tier)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "black", se = FALSE) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = dollar_format()) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "Budget"    = "#fc8181",
    "Mid-Range" = "#4299e1",
    "Premium"   = "#48bb78",
    "Luxury"    = "#ed8936"
  )) +
  labs(
    title = "Sale Price vs Living Area — Colored by Neighborhood Tier",
    subtitle = "Location explains much of the price variation at any given size",
    x = "Above Ground Living Area (sq ft)",
    y = "Sale Price",
    color = "Tier"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# Overall quality vs sale price
ames_clean %>%
  mutate(overall_qual = factor(overall_qual)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = overall_qual, y = sale_price)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "#4299e1", alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = dollar_format()) +
  labs(
    title = "Sale Price by Overall Quality Rating",
    subtitle = "Quality rated 1 (Very Poor) to 10 (Very Excellent)",
    x = "Overall Quality (1-10)",
    y = "Sale Price"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# Year built vs sale price
ggplot(ames_clean, aes(x = year_built, y = sale_price)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, color = "#4299e1") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "red", se = FALSE) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = dollar_format()) +
  labs(
    title = "Sale Price vs Year Built",
    subtitle = "Newer houses tend to sell for more",
    x = "Year Built",
    y = "Sale Price"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# Overall condition vs sale price
ames_clean %>%
  mutate(overall_cond = factor(overall_cond)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = overall_cond, y = sale_price)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "#48bb78", alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = dollar_format()) +
  labs(
    title = "Sale Price by Overall Condition Rating",
    subtitle = "Condition rated 1 (Very Poor) to 10 (Very Excellent)",
    x = "Overall Condition (1-10)",
    y = "Sale Price"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
```{r}
# Full bathrooms vs sale price
ames_clean %>%
  mutate(full_bath = factor(full_bath)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = full_bath, y = sale_price)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "#ed8936", alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = dollar_format()) +
  labs(
    title = "Sale Price by Number of Full Bathrooms",
    subtitle = "Above grade full bathrooms only",
    x = "Number of Full Bathrooms",
    y = "Sale Price"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
```{r}
# Feature Engineering
ames_clean <- ames_clean %>%
  mutate(
    # Log transform target variable
    log_sale_price = log(sale_price),
    
    # Effective year — most recent of built or remodelled
    effective_year = pmax(year_built, year_remod_add)
  )
```


```{r}
# Convert ordinal quality columns to numeric scores
quality_to_num <- function(x) {
  case_when(
    x == "Ex" ~ 5,
    x == "Gd" ~ 4,
    x == "TA" ~ 3,
    x == "Fa" ~ 2,
    x == "Po" ~ 1,
    x == "None" ~ 0,
    TRUE ~ NA_real_
  )
}

ames_clean <- ames_clean %>%
  mutate(across(c(exter_qual, exter_cond, bsmt_qual, bsmt_cond,
                  heating_qc, kitchen_qual, fireplace_qu,
                  garage_qual, garage_cond, pool_qc),
                quality_to_num))
```

```{r}
ames_clean <- ames_clean %>%
  mutate(
    bsmt_exposure = case_when(
      bsmt_exposure == "Gd"   ~ 4,
      bsmt_exposure == "Av"   ~ 3,
      bsmt_exposure == "Mn"   ~ 2,
      bsmt_exposure == "No"   ~ 1,
      bsmt_exposure == "None" ~ 0
    ),
    bsmt_fin_type_1 = case_when(
      bsmt_fin_type_1 == "GLQ"  ~ 6,
      bsmt_fin_type_1 == "ALQ"  ~ 5,
      bsmt_fin_type_1 == "BLQ"  ~ 4,
      bsmt_fin_type_1 == "Rec"  ~ 3,
      bsmt_fin_type_1 == "LwQ"  ~ 2,
      bsmt_fin_type_1 == "Unf"  ~ 1,
      bsmt_fin_type_1 == "None" ~ 0
    ),
    bsmt_fin_type_2 = case_when(
      bsmt_fin_type_2 == "GLQ"  ~ 6,
      bsmt_fin_type_2 == "ALQ"  ~ 5,
      bsmt_fin_type_2 == "BLQ"  ~ 4,
      bsmt_fin_type_2 == "Rec"  ~ 3,
      bsmt_fin_type_2 == "LwQ"  ~ 2,
      bsmt_fin_type_2 == "Unf"  ~ 1,
      bsmt_fin_type_2 == "None" ~ 0
    ),
    functional = case_when(
      functional == "Typ"  ~ 7,
      functional == "Min1" ~ 6,
      functional == "Min2" ~ 5,
      functional == "Mod"  ~ 4,
      functional == "Maj1" ~ 3,
      functional == "Maj2" ~ 2,
      functional == "Sev"  ~ 1,
      functional == "Sal"  ~ 0
    ),
    garage_finish = case_when(
      garage_finish == "Fin"  ~ 3,
      garage_finish == "RFn"  ~ 2,
      garage_finish == "Unf"  ~ 1,
      garage_finish == "None" ~ 0
    ),
    fence = case_when(
      fence == "GdPrv" ~ 4,
      fence == "MnPrv" ~ 3,
      fence == "GdWo"  ~ 2,
      fence == "MnWw"  ~ 1,
      fence == "None"  ~ 0
    ),
    paved_drive = case_when(
      paved_drive == "Y" ~ 2,
      paved_drive == "P" ~ 1,
      paved_drive == "N" ~ 0
    )
  )
```

```{r}
ames_clean %>%
  select(where(is.character)) %>%
  names()
```

```{r}
ames_clean %>% count(sale_condition)
ames_clean %>% count(heating)
ames_clean %>% count(electrical)
ames_clean %>% count(roof_matl)
```
```{r}
ames_clean <- ames_clean %>%
  # Drop near-zero variance columns
  select(-sale_condition, -roof_matl, -heating) %>%
  # Simplify electrical to binary
  mutate(electrical = ifelse(electrical == "SBrkr", 1, 0))
```

```{r}
ames_clean %>% 
  select(where(is.character)) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), n_distinct)) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "column", values_to = "n_categories") %>%
  arrange(desc(n_categories))
```

```{r}
# Convert remaining character columns to numeric/dummy variables
ames_clean <- ames_clean %>%
  
  # Drop columns — redundant or near-zero variance
  select(-neighborhood, -ms_sub_class, -exterior_2nd, 
         -condition_2, -sale_type, -misc_feature, -utilities) %>%
  
  # Convert binary columns
  mutate(
    street      = ifelse(street == "Pave", 1, 0),
    central_air = ifelse(central_air == "Y", 1, 0),
    alley       = ifelse(alley == "None", 0, 1),
    land_slope  = ifelse(land_slope == "Gtl", 1, 0)
  ) %>%
  
  # Simplify exterior_1st to common vs uncommon
  mutate(
    exterior_1st = ifelse(exterior_1st %in% 
                          c("VinylSd", "HdBoard", "MetalSd", 
                            "Wd Sdng", "Plywood"), 
                          exterior_1st, "Other")
  ) %>%
  
  # Convert remaining nominals to dummy variables
  mutate(across(c(ms_zoning, bldg_type, house_style, foundation,
                  garage_type, mas_vnr_type, lot_config, 
                  land_contour, lot_shape, roof_style,
                  condition_1, exterior_1st),
                as.factor))
```

```{r}
# Final feature engineering summary
glimpse(ames_clean)
```
```{r}
# Check low variance columns
ames_clean %>% count(garage_qual)
ames_clean %>% count(garage_cond)
ames_clean %>% count(pool_qc)

```
```{r}
# Drop near-zero variance columns after quality conversion
ames_clean <- ames_clean %>%
  select(-garage_qual, -garage_cond, -pool_qc, -pool_area)
```
